description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, giving it a ribbed appearance. The grooves are deep and regular, creating a strong visual contrast between the raised and recessed areas. The block is solid and has a dark, slightly mottled stone texture, suggesting it is made from a dense material such as granite or basalt. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, with a uniform dark coloration. The overall form is geometric and precise, indicating it may be manufactured or carefully cut. This type of block is often used in urban environments as a barrier, step, or tactile paving element.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves across its top surface and smooth, solid sides. The block has a ribbed, industrial appearance and a dense stone texture.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the grooved side, showing the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: This view highlights the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: The block is viewed from the opposite side of view 0, again showing the horizontal grooves but from the reverse angle.
description_view_3: Similar to view 1, this angle emphasizes the vertical alignment and depth of the grooves on the top surface.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a tactile paving or barrier element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: aligned flush with pavement or adjacent blocks
